task_completion.description: Verifies the agent has joined the correct Jitsi meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows 'http://localhost:8080/TranslatorPlanning' or the Jitsi meeting header displays the room name 'TranslatorPlanning'.

task_completion.description: Verifies the correct poll question is used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Which language pair needs additional interpreter staffing for the trade conference?' is visible in the poll question field or the published poll.

task_completion.description: Verifies all four correct poll options are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The four options 'English - Mandarin Chinese', 'English - Spanish', 'English - Arabic', and 'French - German' are visible as choices in the poll.

task_completion.description: Verifies the poll has been successfully published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The poll appears in a published state (showing radio buttons for voting, a 'Vote' button, or results), rather than in the editable text-input creation form.

task_completion.description: Verifies the Polls panel is left open at the end of the task.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the Jitsi Polls panel/sidebar actively open on the screen displaying the published poll.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent interacted with the Jitsi Meet web interface via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser is open showing the Jitsi Meet UI. The agent did not use a terminal to send API requests (like curl) to create the poll.

integrity.description: Verifies the poll is created inside the actual Jitsi Meet application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The poll is located in the native Jitsi Meet side panel, not on a separate third-party polling website, a local HTML file, or a text editor.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't use browser developer tools to fake the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element/Console) are open in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected URL: http://localhost:8080/TranslatorPlanning. Room name: 'TranslatorPlanning'. Exact poll question: 'Which language pair needs additional interpreter staffing for the trade conference?'. Exact poll options: 'English - Mandarin Chinese', 'English - Spanish', 'English - Arabic', and 'French - German'. The poll must be published (not a draft) and the Jitsi Polls panel must remain open in the final state.
